riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: Customers expect Pylon liable up to 12x fees (or a $2M floor) for data, confidentiality, and security breaches via super-cap structures. Enterprise customers (Coinbase, MX) carve gross negligence, willful misconduct, and fraud out of all caps entirely, creating unlimited Pylon exposure for intentional acts. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: The consequential damages waiver is mutual in form, but customers retain cap protections on their own payment/breach claims while pushing Pylon's data-related exposure into uncapped or super-cap territory. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: MX's dual super-cap (5x/12mo security + 8x/24mo confidentiality) and Coinbase's $2M floor guarantee minimum Pylon exposure regardless of fees paid. Pylon bears asymmetrically higher risk as data sensitivity increases, while Customer's exposure stays pegged to the standard 12-month cap.
standardLang: General Cap: Either party's aggregate liability shall not exceed the fees paid by Customer in the 12 months prior to the act giving rise to the liability (

). Consequential Damages Waiver: Neither party is liable for lost profits, lost revenue, lost data, business interruption, cost of substitute goods/services, or any indirect, incidental, special, exemplary, consequential, or punitive damages. Super-Cap: For claims arising from breach of confidentiality, breach of DPA/BAA, indemnification obligations, or breach of security/privacy obligations, liability is capped at a multiple of the Standard Cap.
fallbackLang: Super-Cap Multiplier: We have agreed to multipliers ranging from 2x (Spherepay) to 8x (MX for confidentiality breaches on 24-month lookback). Standard fallback is 5x (Socure, Osmind, Coinbase, MX for security breaches). Start negotiations at 2x and concede up to 5x. Only go to 6x+ for strategic deals with strong security/financial services requirements. MX's 8x/24-month confidentiality super-cap is the absolute ceiling. Floor Amount: If a customer insists on a dollar floor, $2M has been accepted (Coinbase:

). Spherepay accepted $100K floor. MX accepted $1M floor. Runware (simpler deal): No super-cap was included; standard 12-month cap applied across all claim types. Acceptable for smaller/simpler deals without heavy data processing. Kraken and Prismatic: Standard template deals with 1x/12-month cap, no super-cap. These represent the simplest structures, appropriate for standard-tier customers without complex data processing needs. Unlimited Carve-Outs: Coinbase carved out gross negligence, willful misconduct, fraud, and indemnity obligations entirely from ANY cap. MX carved out gross negligence, willful misconduct, and third-party IP infringement from both the general cap and super-cap. This is the most aggressive position — accept only for strategic enterprise customers.
hardLimit: Never accept unlimited liability for any category except where gross negligence/willful misconduct/fraud carve-outs are specifically negotiated for strategic enterprise deals (Coinbase, MX precedent). Never agree to liability exceeding 8x fees or a 24-month lookback as the super-cap ceiling (MX is the absolute maximum). The consequential damages waiver must always be mutual. Never carve out IP indemnity from the general liability cap unless customer insists and deal value justifies it (Socure, MX precedent).
notes: Carve-outs from both the general cap and consequential damages waiver accepted across deals: bodily injury/death, gross negligence/willful misconduct, fraud/intentional misconduct. Coinbase's $2M floor on the super-cap is the highest accepted — it protects Customer when fees are low relative to data sensitivity. MX introduced a dual super-cap structure: 5x/12-month for security breaches AND 8x/24-month for confidentiality breaches — this is the most complex and most Customer-favorable liability structure in the portfolio. Osmind's 5x super-cap covers BAA breaches (healthcare/PHI), matching the scope used for Coinbase's DPA breaches. Prismatic's single-party liability cap (only Company's liability is capped, not mutual) is Pylon-favorable — use as a starting position for standard deals.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: Customers expect Pylon to defend and hold harmless against third-party IP infringement claims (worldwide for Coinbase and MX), breaches of confidentiality/DPA (Socure, MX), violations of applicable law (Socure, Kraken), and personal data breach remediation costs including credit monitoring (up to 3 years for Upwind, 1 year for Socure). Coinbase explicitly made indemnity obligations uncapped — fully carved out from the liability cap. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: Osmind narrowed Customer's indemnity to only gross negligence/willful misconduct or breach of two specific sections, dramatically reducing what Customers must indemnify Pylon for versus the standard template. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: Removal of standard carve-outs for Customer specifications (Coinbase), worldwide IP coverage (MX, Coinbase), mandatory breach remediation costs (Socure, Upwind), uncapped indemnity exposure (Coinbase), and MX's extension to cover breach of all Section 3 obligations create the most expansive indemnification profile in the portfolio.
standardLang: Pylon Indemnification: Pylon will indemnify, defend, and hold Customer harmless from third-party claims alleging that the Services infringe any U.S. intellectual property rights (copyright, trademark, trade secret). Standard exclusions apply: (i) combination with non-Pylon products, (ii) Customer modifications, (iii) use outside Documentation/Agreement, (iv) continued use after notice of infringement, (v) compliance with Customer's designs/specifications. IP Remedy: If Services are found infringing, Pylon may at its option: (a) replace/modify to be non-infringing with substantially similar functionality, (b) obtain a license for Customer, or (c) if neither is commercially practicable, terminate and refund prepaid unused fees. Customer Indemnification: Customer will indemnify Pylon from third-party claims arising from: (a) Customer's breach of the Agreement/restrictions, (b) violation of third-party IP/privacy rights related to Customer Data, (c) violation of applicable law, (d) gross negligence or willful misconduct.
fallbackLang: Broader Pylon Indemnity Triggers: For strategic customers, expanded beyond IP to include: gross negligence/willful misconduct (all deals), breach of confidentiality/DPA (Socure, Upwind, MX), violation of law (Socure, Kraken, Prismatic), subcontractor compliance (Spherepay), misuse of Customer Data by third-party AI providers (Upwind). MX and Coinbase: Indemnity for IP infringement extended to cover ALL third-party IP rights worldwide (not just U.S. patents/copyrights/trade secrets). MX also covers breach of Section 3 obligations (confidentiality, data, AI). Coinbase: IP indemnity does not include standard carve-outs for Customer specifications — this is more Pylon-favorable as it removes an exception customers typically negotiate. However, indemnity obligations are explicitly uncapped (carved out from liability cap per Section 7.2.3). Prismatic: Broadest Pylon indemnity — covers breach of Terms, IP infringement, violation of law, AND gross negligence/willful misconduct. This is the most expansive template indemnity. Personal Data Breach Remediation: Accepted for Upwind and Socure. Pylon reimburses costs for breach notifications, credit monitoring (1 year Socure, 3 years Upwind). Subject to Super-Cap.
hardLimit: Never accept indemnification for claims arising from Customer's own specifications/designs (except where strategically waived as in Coinbase). Never accept unlimited indemnification exposure without explicit deal-level approval — Coinbase's uncapped indemnity is the ceiling. The IP indemnity remedy must always include the option to terminate and refund as a last resort. Never agree to indemnify for punitive damages or damages beyond what's paid to third parties.
notes: Twingate's deal requires a signed paid order form as a condition for Pylon's indemnification — maintain this standard. MX's indemnity for breach of Section 3 obligations (confidentiality/data/AI) is unique and goes beyond standard IP-only indemnity. Osmind's Customer indemnity is narrowly scoped: only gross negligence/willful misconduct in violation of the Agreement, or material breach of Sections 2.1 (Restrictions) or 3.2 (Customer Data representations). This is the most Customer-favorable limitation on Customer's indemnity obligations. Kraken's indemnity structure mirrors the standard template with IP + gross negligence/willful misconduct + violation of law from Pylon.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: Customers expect to own all Customer Data and AI inputs/outputs, creating liability for Pylon if it uses, retains, or discloses Customer Data outside the scope of Services. Pylon's primary exposure here flows into the confidentiality and indemnification sections rather than directly from ownership terms. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: Customers have significantly restricted Pylon's commercial data rights — MX and Osmind exclude Customer Data and Confidential Information from both Feedback and Usage Data definitions, and all enterprise deals prohibit AI training on Customer Data. MX's prohibition extends to resale or distribution of Customer Data in any format, including anonymized/aggregated, to any third party. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: AI content ownership (AI Input and Output as Customer Data) creates ongoing obligations to treat LLM outputs as Customer property. MX's subcontractor prohibition on AI training means Pylon must contractually bind its AI providers, creating pass-through compliance liability Pylon cannot fully control.
standardLang: Customer Ownership: Customer retains all right, title, and interest in Customer Data. Pylon Ownership: Pylon owns and retains all rights in (a) Services, Software, Documentation, and all improvements/enhancements/modifications, (b) software/technology developed in connection with implementation/support, (c) all related IP rights. Feedback: Customer grants Pylon rights to use feedback. Usage Data: Pylon may collect and use anonymized, de-identified, aggregated usage data to improve Services.
fallbackLang: Feedback Restrictions: Standard is broad feedback license (Twingate, Runware, Kraken, Prismatic). Upwind limited feedback to exclude Proprietary Information. MX explicitly excludes Customer Data and Confidential Information from Feedback definition. Use MX's clean exclusion as standard for enterprise deals. Usage Data Definition: MX has the strongest exclusion — Usage Data explicitly excludes both Customer Data AND Customer Confidential Information, and must be anonymized, de-identified, AND aggregated such that it cannot identify Customer, its Authorized Users, any client of Customer, or any natural person. This is the gold standard. Osmind matches this scope. Kraken/Prismatic: Standard template version allows Pylon to collect data

— this broader scope is Pylon-favorable but may be challenged by enterprise customers. No Model Training: All enterprise deals now prohibit AI training on Customer Data. MX has the strongest version — represents and warrants that it will not permit subcontractors or any other party to resell, distribute, or use Customer Data to train/improve AI models. AI Content Ownership: AI Input and Output are Customer Data owned by Customer (Osmind, MX, Coinbase). Include in all new agreements. Customer Data License: MX limits to non-transferable and
Coinbase's license scope is standard. Kraken/Prismatic use the broader transferable, sublicensable version.
hardLimit: Pylon must always retain ownership of Services, Software, and all derivative works. Never grant Customer ownership of improvements/enhancements. Never allow Customer Data license to survive termination (except archival/legal). Never agree to assign IP developed during implementation to Customer.
notes: MX's combined restrictions (no resale of any Customer Data, no AI training, explicit exclusion from Feedback and Usage Data) represent the most protective Customer data framework. Osmind's Affiliate provision allows Customer's subsidiaries to enter Order Forms under the same Agreement — standard for enterprise deals. Coinbase's AI prohibition includes requirement for Third-Party AI providers to maintain ZDR agreements.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: Customers expect Pylon liable for confidentiality breaches for survival periods ranging from 5 years (standard) to indefinite (Twingate, Socure). MX subjects confidentiality breaches to the highest super-cap in the portfolio — 8x fees on a 24-month lookback. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: Customers retain standard exclusions (public domain, prior knowledge, independent development) and generally face the same mutual obligations, but enterprise customers push for more aggressive return/destruction timelines and broader compelled disclosure cooperation — obligations that fall more heavily on Pylon as the data processor. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: Coinbase's 10-day return/destruction deadline is the most aggressive in the portfolio. MX requires deletion from all vendor and subcontractor systems within 30 days (max 1-year retention for legal holds), extending Pylon's deletion obligation to its entire supply chain. MX's 'reasonable cooperation' for compelled disclosure and indefinite survival effectively make Pylon's confidentiality obligations permanent with no sunset.
standardLang: Definition: Proprietary/Confidential Information includes business, technical, or financial information of each party. Pylon's includes non-public information about Services/Software. Customer's includes Customer Data. Obligations: Receiving party will (i) use commercially reasonable precautions (not less than used for own confidential info, and no less than a reasonable degree of care), (ii) not use except in performance of Services, (iii) not disclose to third parties. Standard Exclusions: Information that (a) is/becomes publicly available, (b) was already known, (c) rightfully disclosed by third party, (d) independently developed. Compelled Disclosure: Receiving party may disclose if required by law, with reasonable notice.
fallbackLang: Survival Period: 5 years post-disclosure (Upwind, Spherepay, Kraken, Prismatic standard template), indefinite (Twingate, Socure). Osmind, MX, and Coinbase do not specify a survival period in the confidentiality section — treat as indefinite during term, standard 5 years post-termination. Permitted Disclosees: Osmind, MX, and Coinbase add that receiving party may disclose to employees/contractors/agents with need-to-know who are bound by obligations at least as restrictive. Return/Destruction: Coinbase requires return/destruction within 10 days post-termination (most aggressive). MX requires 30-day deletion from all systems including vendors/subcontractors with maximum 1-year retention for legal holds. Standard is 30 days. Protective Orders: Upwind requires
for protective orders. MX requires
to limit compelled disclosure. Standard is
to notify.
hardLimit: Never agree to obligations that would prevent Pylon from using anonymized/aggregated usage data. Customer Data must always be defined as Customer's Proprietary/Confidential Information. The standard exclusions (public domain, prior knowledge, independent development, third-party disclosure) must always be included. Never accept return/destruction timelines shorter than 10 days.
notes: Coinbase's 10-day return/destruction timeline is the most aggressive in the portfolio. MX's requirement to delete from vendor/subcontractor systems within 30 days (with 1-year max retention for legal holds) represents the most comprehensive deletion scope. Osmind includes a HIPAA-specific carve-out — Pylon's obligations under the BAA remain in force even during Evaluation/Beta Services. This is important for healthcare customers.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: Customers expect Pylon liable for security incidents with notification windows of 24–72 hours, breach remediation costs (Upwind: 3 years credit monitoring, Socure: 1 year), and ZDR failures by third-party AI providers. MX's in-line security requirements (NIST/ISO 27001, encryption, MFA, annual pen testing with executive summary sharing) create broad ongoing compliance obligations. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: Customers bear no corresponding security obligations to Pylon. The DPA framework is entirely one-directional — Pylon assumes all data processor obligations while Customer acts solely as controller. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: Coinbase's 50-page DPA with multi-jurisdiction GDPR terms is the most expansive regulatory framework accepted. MX's AI training prohibition extends to aggregated and anonymized data and covers all subcontractors, creating pass-through compliance liability. ZDR commitments with AI providers represent contractual obligations Pylon cannot fully guarantee, and any provider-side failure becomes Pylon's direct breach.
standardLang: Third-Party AI: Pylon uses third-party AI providers in real-time to deliver Services. AI providers operate under zero data retention (ZDR) agreements and do not store Customer Data or train on it. Usage Data: Pylon collects anonymized/aggregated usage data that cannot identify Customer or any end user. Customer Data License: Non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free license to use Customer Data solely to provide Services. AI Features: AI Input and Output are Customer Data, owned by Customer. Customer responsible for evaluating accuracy.
fallbackLang: DPA Attachment: For customers requiring formal DPA (Upwind, Socure, Coinbase), attach as exhibit. Coinbase includes comprehensive 50-page DPA with GDPR multi-jurisdiction terms plus country-specific annexes (Australia, Singapore, US/CCPA, Canada, Brazil). MX: Comprehensive data security section (Section 3.7) with NIST/ISO 27001 requirements, encryption (transit + rest), MFA, intrusion detection, vulnerability management, and employee training. This is the most detailed in-line security section without a separate DPA. Security Incident Notification: Ranges from 24 hours (Upwind for critical incidents) to 48 hours (Twingate, Socure, MX) to 72 hours (Coinbase DSE). Standard offer is 48 hours. AI Training Prohibition: All deals now include explicit prohibition on using Customer Data for AI training. MX has the strongest version — prohibits resale/distribution of Customer Data in any format including aggregated/anonymized to any third party, and prohibits training except for fine-tuning exclusively for Customer's benefit with irreversible anonymization. Customer Data License Restrictions: Upwind required non-transferable, non-sublicensable, revocable. MX limits license to

Standard version allows transferable, sublicensable. Restrict for enterprise customers. Osmind BAA: HIPAA Subcontractor Business Associate Agreement — only applicable for healthcare customers processing PHI. Maintains compliance even during Evaluation/Beta Services.
hardLimit: ZDR agreements with AI providers must be maintained throughout the Term — no exceptions. Never agree to give Customer audit rights over Pylon's AI provider contracts (only summary results). Pylon must never sell, monetize, or share Customer Data with third parties. Never agree to process Customer Data outside the United States without written consent.
notes: Coinbase's DSE (Data and Security Exhibit) is a 21-page standalone security requirements document — the most comprehensive security attachment in the portfolio. MX's in-line data security section (3.7) is the most detailed MSA-embedded security framework. Osmind's BAA is unique to healthcare customers. MX requires annual penetration testing with executive summary results shared — this goes beyond standard SOC 2 requirements. Kraken and Prismatic use the standard template ZDR/Third-Party AI provision without formal DPA or security exhibits.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: Customers expect a pro-rata refund of prepaid fees upon Pylon's material breach. MX extends this to a full refund of ALL fees if Services were never deployable — eliminating any benefit Pylon received. MX can treat Section 3 (confidentiality/data/AI) breaches as non-curable, triggering a 5-business-day termination right with no opportunity to cure — the most aggressive termination provision in the portfolio. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: Customers secured refund rights without paying through the full term if Pylon breaches, while Pylon retains no equivalent refund right if Customer terminates early without cause. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: MX's non-curable breach right converts any confidentiality/data/AI violation into an immediate contract termination risk with simultaneous full financial exposure. The 30-day deletion requirement covering all vendors and subcontractors, combined with continuing audit rights during retention, creates ongoing post-termination obligations that extend well beyond the agreement end date.
standardLang: Initial Term: As specified in Order Form. Renewal: After Initial Term, renewal requires Customer written consent for additional 1-year periods. Standard is NO auto-renewal (Twingate, Runware, Socure, Kraken, Coinbase). Termination for Cause: Either party may terminate upon 30 days' written notice if the other party materially breaches and fails to cure. Effect: All access rights cease, Customer pays through termination date, return/destroy Proprietary Information.
fallbackLang: Auto-Renewal: Spherepay (30-day opt-out), Prismatic (90-day opt-out), Osmind (30-day opt-out). Standard is opt-in renewal. Accept auto-renewal only if Customer specifically requests it. Prismatic's 90-day notice is the longest opt-out window. No Auto-Renewal (Strictest): Coinbase requires a NEW signed Order Form to renew — no automatic continuation. Kraken requires Customer to affirmatively provide 30 days' notice of intent to renew. MX requires

to renew — effectively mutual opt-in. Non-Curable Breach: MX (Section 5.3.1) allows Customer to treat Pylon's breach of Section 3 (confidentiality/data/AI) as non-curable, with termination on 5 business days' notice. This is the most aggressive Customer termination right. Refund on Termination: If Customer terminates for Pylon's breach, pro-rata refund of prepaid unused fees (all deals). MX adds: if Services were never deployable, full refund of ALL fees. Data Deletion: MX requires deletion from ALL systems including vendors/subcontractors within 30 days, with maximum 1-year retention for legal holds and continuing audit rights during retention. Coinbase: 30-day data export plus 30-day transition services at then-current rates. Suspension Grace: Ranges from 5 business days (Prismatic, Spherepay) to 15 business days (Upwind). Standard is 10 business days.
hardLimit: Never agree to termination for convenience by Customer without payment through end of current term. Never agree to cure periods shorter than 15 days for non-payment or 30 days for other material breaches. Never accept non-curable breach provisions broader than Section 3 (confidentiality/data) without specific deal approval. Pylon's suspension rights must always be preserved.
notes: MX's non-curable breach right for Section 3 violations is the most aggressive termination provision in the portfolio. Coinbase's requirement for a new signed Order Form to renew is the most restrictive renewal mechanism. Prismatic's 90-day auto-renewal opt-out notice is the longest notice period. MX's requirement to delete from vendor/subcontractor systems is the most comprehensive deletion obligation. Osmind includes insolvency termination with 90-day dismissal window — standard for enterprise deals.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: Customers expect Pylon to warrant that Services perform materially per Documentation, with a sole remedy of correction, replacement, or refund of prepaid unused fees. MX extended the refund remedy to ALL fees paid if Services were never deployable — removing the 'unused' limitation and potentially requiring full contract value reimbursement. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: Customers bear no performance warranties to Pylon; the warranty framework is entirely one-directional. The 'AS IS' disclaimer is mutual in form but has no practical value to Pylon since Pylon is the service provider. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: Upwind's expanded warranty — covering no open-source obligations, no restrictions on Customer's use, and no legal/financial liability beyond the Agreement — goes beyond Documentation compliance into broad legal assurances. MX's full-fee refund for non-deployable Services eliminates the standard ceiling on warranty exposure.
standardLang: Performance Warranty: Services will perform in material conformance with Documentation and applicable SLA. Sole Remedy: Error-correction, replacement, or if commercially impracticable, terminate and refund prepaid unused fees. Disclaimer: Except for express warranties, Services provided
fallbackLang: Extended Warranty Remedy (MX): If Services were unable to be deployed in a conforming manner, refund of ALL fees (not just prepaid unused). This is the most Customer-favorable remedy. Broader Warranty (Upwind): Expanded to include no open-source obligations, won't restrict Customer's use, won't cause legal/financial liability beyond Agreement. Avoid unless deal-critical. Conformance Warranty with Cure Period (Twingate): 30-day cure period; if Pylon cannot substantially correct, Customer may terminate and receive refund. Malicious Code Warranty (Twingate): Pylon will prevent and promptly remove Malicious Code. Acceptable to include. Kraken, Prismatic, Osmind: Standard template warranty — material compliance with Documentation, sole remedy at Pylon's discretion. Coinbase: Standard performance warranty, no additional warranties beyond Documentation compliance.
hardLimit: Never warrant uninterrupted or error-free service. Never warrant results or suitability beyond Documentation. The
disclaimer must always be included. Never accept unlimited warranty remedies.
notes: refund for non-deployable Services is unique and should only be offered where deployment failure risk is real. All agreements consistently disclaim UCC, UCITA, and UN Convention. Prismatic includes a broader AS-IS disclaimer with additional categories: accuracy, results of use, reliability, privacy, security, quiet enjoyment.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: MX requires Pylon to maintain $5M Tech E&O/Cyber Risk, $5M Umbrella/Excess, $1M CGL, and $1M Crime insurance as binding contractual obligations — failure constitutes a material breach. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: Customers face no corresponding insurance obligations to Pylon. All insurance requirements are entirely one-directional. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: Additional insured status on CGL and Umbrella, waiver of subrogation, 3-year tail coverage on claims-made policies, and 30-day cancellation notice are all obligations solely on Pylon. The Crime insurance requirement with Third-Party Coverage extension is unique — it specifically covers Customer data and asset theft by Pylon personnel or subcontractors, converting an insurance procurement obligation into a direct risk indemnification mechanism. MX's A.M. Best A- VII rating requirement limits insurer selection and may increase premium costs.
standardLang: No insurance requirements have been included in most executed MSAs. Insurance provisions are not standard in Pylon's contracts.
fallbackLang: MX Insurance (Exhibit C) — NEW CEILING: The most comprehensive insurance requirement in the portfolio. Required coverages: (1) Tech E&O / Cyber Risk: $5M per claim and aggregate, covering professional negligence, data breach (forensics, legal, notification), regulatory fines, unauthorized access to Customer Data. (2) Commercial General Liability: $1M per occurrence / $2M aggregate. (3) Umbrella/Excess Liability: $5M per occurrence, follow-form over CGL and Professional/Cyber. (4) Crime Insurance: $1M with Third-Party Coverage extension for Customer data/asset theft. Policy Conditions: Additional Insured status for Customer on all policies (except E&O and Crime), waiver of subrogation, 3-year tail coverage for claims-made policies, 30-day notice of cancellation/material reduction. Coinbase, Kraken, Osmind, Prismatic: No insurance requirements.
hardLimit: Never agree to insurance requirements disproportionate to contract value. Never agree to name Customer as additional insured on ALL policies (only CGL and Umbrella is typical). Reject coverage amounts exceeding MX's requirements without specific deal approval. 3-year tail coverage is the maximum.
notes: MX is the only deal with formal insurance requirements — reflects its position as a financial services company. MX requires A.M. Best A- VII or higher rated insurers. Insurance provisions are increasingly requested by financial services and regulated industry customers. Use MX's Exhibit C as the template when insurance is required.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: Minimal direct financial exposure — the standard remedy for unauthorized use of Customer marks is removal upon notice, with no damages. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: Enterprise customers (Coinbase, MX, Osmind) require prior written consent per instance, effectively eliminating Pylon's ability to use the customer relationship for marketing without active, case-by-case approval. Coinbase's brand guidelines requirement adds an ongoing compliance obligation. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: The restriction is asymmetric — customers face no corresponding limitation on referencing Pylon. Coinbase's brand guidelines requirement converts a consent-based restriction into a compliance framework Pylon must actively manage. The practical impact is commercial rather than financial: Pylon loses the ability to leverage enterprise logos for pipeline development, which has indirect revenue consequences beyond the contract itself.
standardLang: Upon Customer's written consent (on a case-by-case basis), Pylon may use Customer's name, logo, trademarks, and trade names in marketing materials. All other public statements require mutual consent.
fallbackLang: Strict Prior Written Approval: Coinbase, Osmind, MX all require

Coinbase additionally subjects all usage to Coinbase's brand guidelines. This is the most restrictive tier. Mutual Consent Required: MX requires mutual consent for ALL public statements (even beyond logo/name use). Automatic with Opt-Out: Spherepay and Prismatic grant Pylon automatic right to use name/logo, with Customer able to withdraw consent upon notice. Kraken uses same template as Prismatic — automatic grant with reference account cooperation. Reference Account: Spherepay, Socure, Kraken, and Prismatic include
Upwind softens to
hardLimit: Never agree to blanket restrictions preventing Pylon from ever identifying the customer relationship. At minimum, Pylon should be able to list Customer's name in investor/financial reporting. Never agree to damages/penalties for accidental publicity violations — remedy should be removal upon notice.
notes: Enterprise trend is moving toward strict prior written consent per instance (Coinbase/Osmind/MX model). Coinbase's brand guidelines requirement is unique. Standard template (Kraken/Prismatic) auto-grants publicity rights — be prepared for enterprise customers to push back. MX's language appears to have a drafting error with repeated/conflicting clauses — the intent is prior written consent required.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: Direct financial exposure is low. Primary risk is revenue timing (Net 30–60 terms delay cash collection) and potential loss of payment rights through procedural non-compliance. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: Customers systematically reduced their own payment obligations via extended terms (up to Net 60), 60-day dispute windows, and fee increase caps (Upwind: 5% annual cap). Coinbase's PO requirement creates a payment-gating mechanism — if Pylon commences Services without a valid PO or correct invoice-to address, Coinbase has no payment obligation. This shifts the burden of administrative compliance entirely onto Pylon. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: Coinbase's PO-gated service commencement is the most asymmetric provision: Pylon must affirmatively manage invoice formatting and PO procurement or forfeit payment rights. Upwind's 5% fee cap limits Pylon's ability to recover cost increases at renewal, effectively locking in pricing against inflation. The combination of extended dispute windows (60 days) and late interest caps (as low as 0.5% for Upwind) meaningfully reduces the cost of late payment for customers.
standardLang:
Invoice Terms: Net 30 days from invoice date. Late Payment Interest: 1.5% per month on outstanding balance (or max permitted by law). Billing Disputes: Customer must raise within defined window. Taxes: Customer responsible for all applicable taxes except taxes on Pylon's net income. Fee Changes: Upon prior written notice, effective at next renewal.

Customer is responsible for payment of all sales, use and excise taxes applicable to Fees and other services fees (

), including but not limited to, all sales, use or other taxes, which may be levied or assessed in connection with this Agreement, except for taxes based upon Pylon's income. Fees are exclusive of Taxes.
fallbackLang: Extended Payment Terms: Net 45 days (MX). Net 60 days (Upwind, Socure). Standard is Net 30; accept Net 45-60 for enterprise customers. Billing Dispute Windows: 10 days (Spherepay, Kraken, Prismatic), 21 days (Socure), 60 days (Upwind, Osmind, MX, Coinbase). Standard offer is 14 days. PO Requirements: Coinbase requires both signed Order Form AND PO number before Pylon can commence Services. Invoices must include PO number and correct

address or Coinbase has no payment obligation. This is unique to Coinbase. Late Payment Interest: Ranges from 0.5% (Upwind) to 1% (Kraken) to 1.5% (standard). Coinbase does not specify late interest. Suspension Grace: 5 business days (Prismatic, Spherepay), 10 business days (standard, Kraken, Osmind, MX, Coinbase), 15 business days (Upwind). Fee Increase Caps: Upwind capped at 5% annual. Standard is no cap.
hardLimit: Never accept payment terms longer than Net 60. Never waive right to charge late payment interest. Never accept PO requirements that would allow Customer to avoid payment by not issuing PO after signing Order Form. Pylon must always retain right to suspend for non-payment.
notes: Coinbase's PO-gated service commencement is unique and creates operational complexity — flag in onboarding. MX's Net 45 is a middle-ground option between Net 30 and Net 60. Kraken's 1% late interest rate is lower than the 1.5% standard — minor concession. Osmind and MX explicitly state
— include this protective language in all deals.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: Exposure here is procedural and cost-based rather than substantive. The governing law selection determines where disputes are adjudicated and what legal standards apply, directly affecting litigation costs and enforcement complexity. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: Mandatory arbitration clauses (Socure, Prismatic) with class action waivers reduce mass litigation risk for both parties, but arbitration can disadvantage the party with smaller claims — typically Pylon in fee disputes. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: Kraken's England and Wales governing law is the only non-U.S. law accepted and creates cross-border enforcement complexity for a Delaware-incorporated company. Pylon bears disproportionate enforcement burden under foreign law. Customers who secured their home jurisdiction (California for Twingate, Spherepay, Prismatic) gain a practical litigation advantage over Pylon, which must litigate away from its preferred Delaware venue.
standardLang: Delaware law, without regard to conflict of laws provisions. Exclusive venue in federal or state courts in New Castle County, Delaware. Both parties waive right to jury trial.
fallbackLang: California Law: Accepted for Twingate, Spherepay, and Prismatic. Delaware is preferred; California is acceptable fallback. England and Wales: Accepted for Kraken. This is the first non-U.S. governing law in the portfolio — reflects Kraken's international structure. Mandatory Arbitration: Socure and Prismatic include mandatory individual arbitration with class action waiver. Prismatic specifically states this in a prominent notice at the top of the agreement. Coinbase: Delaware law with New Castle County venue and jury trial waiver (preferred). No Specified Venue: Kraken and Prismatic do not specify an exclusive venue — only governing law. Prevailing Party: All agreements include prevailing party recovery of costs and attorneys' fees.
hardLimit: Preferred jurisdictions: Delaware, California, New York. Accept England and Wales only for UK-based customers. Jury trial waiver must always be included. Never agree to mandatory arbitration in a jurisdiction where Pylon has no presence unless reciprocal.
notes: Kraken's England and Wales governing law is the only non-U.S. law accepted. This may create enforcement complexity and should be flagged for legal review on future international deals. Prismatic's prominent arbitration notice at the top of the agreement is a good drafting practice when arbitration is included.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: No current exposure — non-solicitation provisions are absent from all 10 executed MSAs including enterprise deals with Coinbase and MX. No scope shift in either direction. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: N/A. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: None at present. If requested in future negotiations, any provision must be mutual and limited to 12 months post-termination to avoid asymmetric hiring restrictions.
standardLang: No non-solicitation provisions have been included in any of the 10 executed MSAs reviewed. This is not a standard provision in Pylon's contracts.
fallbackLang: If requested by Customer, consider a mutual non-solicitation of key personnel for the term plus 12 months, limited to direct solicitation (not general recruitment advertising). Ensure any non-solicit is mutual.
hardLimit: Never accept non-solicitation periods longer than 12 months post-termination. Must be mutual. Never restrict hiring from general job postings.
notes: Non-solicitation remains absent from all 10 deals including enterprise customers like Coinbase and MX. This confirms it is not an industry expectation for SaaS contracts.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: Customers (Coinbase) expect no obligation to pay fees during force majeure events that prevent Services — creating direct revenue loss risk for Pylon during prolonged disruptions without any corresponding Customer obligation. Upwind expects a full refund after 30+ days of force majeure. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: Payment obligations are excluded from force majeure protection in all deals, meaning customers can always invoke force majeure to avoid paying fees but Pylon cannot invoke it to delay payment obligations it owes. This is structurally asymmetric. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: Coinbase's fee suspension provision is the most aggressive — it converts a force majeure event into a unilateral fee holiday for Customer with no equivalent right for Pylon. Upwind's 30-day termination trigger with full refund means a single prolonged outage (regardless of cause) creates simultaneous termination and full financial exposure for Pylon.
standardLang: Neither party is liable for delay or failure to perform (except payment of fees) due to events beyond reasonable control: natural disaster, civil disturbance, terrorism/war, strike, lockout, epidemic, third-party provider failure, governmental action. Both parties will use reasonable efforts to mitigate.
fallbackLang: Termination Right for Extended Force Majeure: Upwind includes right to terminate and receive full refund if force majeure prevents Services for 30+ days. Coinbase: Customer NOT required to pay fees during force majeure preventing Services (Section 9.11). This is more Customer-favorable than standard. Cure Obligation: Osmind, MX, Kraken, and Prismatic require the affected party to cure the breach

after the force majeure event. Standard template language. No Termination Trigger: Coinbase, Kraken, Osmind, MX, and Prismatic do not include a termination right for extended force majeure. Standard is no automatic termination right.
hardLimit: Payment obligations must always be excluded from force majeure. Never accept force majeure termination triggers shorter than 30 consecutive days. Force majeure must always be mutual.
notes: Coinbase's provision excusing Customer from paying fees during force majeure affecting Services is unique and more Customer-favorable than other deals. Upwind's 30-day termination trigger remains the ceiling for strategic deals only. Epidemic/pandemic consistently listed across all 10 agreements.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: Direct liability exposure is low — unauthorized assignment constitutes a material breach but no independent indemnification obligation arises. The primary risk is deal continuity in M&A scenarios. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: Four deals (Spherepay, Socure, Kraken, Prismatic) allow Pylon to assign freely while requiring Customer to obtain Pylon's written consent — Pylon-favorable. However, four enterprise deals (Upwind, Osmind, MX, Coinbase) require mutual consent except for M&A, eliminating Pylon's unilateral assignment right in those relationships. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: Mutual consent requirements in enterprise deals create deal contingencies during Pylon's own M&A or restructuring — Customer's consent right could be used as leverage. The M&A carve-out preserves the most critical assignment scenario, but Affiliate transfers and subsidiary restructuring require explicit consent in deals without the Affiliate carve-out (Osmind and MX have it; others may not).
standardLang: Neither party may assign without written consent of the other, except either party may assign without consent in connection with merger, change of control, sale of substantially all assets, or similar transaction.
fallbackLang: Pylon-Only Free Assignment: Spherepay, Socure, Kraken, and Prismatic allow Pylon to assign freely without consent, while Customer requires Pylon's written consent. Most Pylon-favorable version. Mutual Free Assignment: Twingate allows either party to assign without consent. Customer-favorable. Mutual with M&A Exception: Upwind, Osmind, MX, and Coinbase require mutual consent except for M&A/change of control/Affiliate transfers. This is the balanced approach. Affiliate Assignment: Osmind and MX explicitly allow assignment to Affiliates without consent, in addition to M&A. Successor Binding: Osmind and MX include
hardLimit: Pylon must always retain the right to assign in M&A transactions without Customer consent. Never agree to assignment restrictions that would prevent restructuring. If Customer requires consent for Pylon's assignment, ensure
notes: The portfolio splits roughly evenly: 4 deals with Pylon-only free assignment (Pylon-favorable) vs. 4 deals with mutual consent + M&A exception (balanced). Coinbase uses the balanced mutual approach consistent with its enterprise position. Osmind and MX's explicit Affiliate carve-out is useful for customers with subsidiary structures.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: Customers expect Pylon to cover audit costs if material non-compliance is found (MX). Post-security incident audits are unlimited in frequency, meaning a single breach triggers an indefinite audit right that is not counted against the annual limit. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: Customers bear no corresponding audit obligations to Pylon — the audit framework is entirely one-directional. Customers face no access costs, no audit caps, and no reciprocal disclosure requirements. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: MX's audit framework is the most comprehensive: on-site access rights + SOC 2/ISO 27001/PCI DSS compliance + annual pen testing with executive summary sharing + cost-shifting if material non-compliance is found. Cost-shifting provisions mean a compliance finding triggers both remediation costs and audit expense reimbursement simultaneously. Coinbase's document-only approach (no on-site) is more Pylon-favorable and should be the starting negotiation position for all enterprise deals.
standardLang: SOC 2 Type II: Pylon maintains and provides annual SOC 2 Type II report (or ISO 27001) upon request, no more than once per calendar year. Penetration Testing: Annual third-party pen test with summary results available.
fallbackLang: On-Site Audit Rights (MX, Socure): Customer may conduct or commission audits of Pylon's practices/records. MX: 10 business days' advance notice, once per year (except following Security Incident), during business hours, at Customer's cost (unless audit reveals material non-compliance, then Pylon pays). MX additionally requires Pylon to accept SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, or other financial services industry-standard audit processes AND annual penetration testing with executive summary shared. Document-Only Audit (Coinbase DSE): Annual request for Security Risk Assessment Artifacts. NO on-site audits. This is the most Pylon-favorable audit framework among enterprise customers. Post-Security Incident: All enterprise deals allow additional audit/artifact requests following a Security Incident (not counted toward annual limit). Vulnerability Scanning (Socure DPA): Annual scans with 30-day remediation for high/critical vulnerabilities. Kraken, Prismatic: No audit rights provisions beyond standard SOC 2 availability. Osmind: No specific audit provisions in the MSA.
hardLimit: Maximum frequency once per calendar year (with Security Incident exception). Customer must bear audit costs unless material breach found. Auditors must be pre-approved by Pylon. Scope limited to security, confidentiality, and data protection. Never allow results shared with third parties without consent.
notes: MX's audit rights are the most comprehensive: on-site rights + SOC 2/ISO 27001/PCI DSS + annual pen testing + executive summary sharing + cost-shifting on material non-compliance findings. Coinbase's document-only approach (no on-site) is Pylon-favorable and should be the starting position for enterprise deals. Socure's DPA adds vulnerability scanning requirements beyond standard audit rights. MX requires PCI DSS as an option — reflects financial services industry standards.

riskSummary: PYLON LIABILITY: Customers expect service credits of 5% per 30-minute downtime increment beyond 1 hour monthly, capped at 25%–50% of monthly fees depending on the deal. Twingate can terminate the entire agreement after 2 severe downtime events (>3 hours) in 12 months or failure to maintain 99.9% uptime for any 3 months in a rolling 12-month period — converting a service performance issue into full contract termination risk. CUSTOMER SCOPE REDUCTION: Credits are the sole and exclusive remedy in most deals (except Twingate's termination right), capping Pylon's SLA exposure at a fraction of monthly fees. Customers bear no corresponding performance obligations to Pylon. SCOPE EXPANDED ON PYLON: Upwind's tiered credit structure (up to 35% for <95% uptime) with automatic credit application and a 90-day notification window represent the most Customer-favorable SLA terms. Automatic credits eliminate the requirement for Customer to claim — Pylon must proactively apply them. Twingate's termination trigger is the single most consequential expansion of SLA risk: a repeated service reliability issue becomes a contract termination event with no financial ceiling on the resulting exposure.
standardLang: Uptime: 99.9% monthly availability. Exclusions: Scheduled maintenance, Customer-caused downtime, third-party outages, Customer breach. Credits: 5% of monthly fees per 30 consecutive minutes of downtime exceeding 1 hour. Maximum 25% per month. Notification: 24 hours to claim credit. Support: Slack/email, 9am-5pm PT weekdays, 1 business day response. Sole and exclusive remedy.
fallbackLang: Higher Uptime Target: Twingate at 99.95%. Standard is 99.9%. All other deals use 99.9%. Credit Structures: Simple (Kraken, Prismatic, Osmind, Coinbase): 5% per 30 minutes, max 25%/month. Tiered (Socure): <99.9% = 5%; <99% = 15%; <95% = 35%. Most Generous (Upwind): <99.9% = 5%; <99% = 15%; <98% = 30%, applied to 1/12 annual fees, automatic application. Highest Cap: Spherepay and Twingate at 50%/month. Notification Windows: 24 hours (Kraken, Prismatic, MX standard), 5 business days (Osmind), 30 days (Socure), 90 days (Upwind). Shorter windows favor Pylon. Termination Trigger (Twingate): Customer may terminate if 2 events of Severe Downtime (>3 hours) in 12 months or <99.9% for any 3 months in 12 months. Support Hours: Basic (9-5 PT): Spherepay, Runware, Kraken, Prismatic, Osmind, Coinbase. Extended (7am-7pm PT): Twingate standard. Enterprise 24/7: Upwind, Socure. Coinbase Support: Slack/email only, 1 business day response — basic tier despite enterprise contract. Scheduled Maintenance: 5 business days' notice (Socure, Twingate) to 10 days (Upwind). Upwind caps at 4 hours/month, Sundays 2-7am ET only. Response Times: 30 min critical (Upwind), 1 hour SEV-1 (Twingate), 1 business day standard (all others).
hardLimit: Never commit to uptime higher than 99.95%. Never agree to credits exceeding 50% of monthly fees. Credits must be sole and exclusive remedy (except termination for prolonged outages). Never agree to automatic cash refunds — credits against future invoices only. Never accept downtime measurement by Customer alone.
notes: Twingate's termination trigger for repeated severe downtime remains the most aggressive SLA consequence. Coinbase negotiated an enterprise MSA with basic-tier support (9-5 PT, 1 business day) — demonstrates that SLA/support tier is independent of contract sophistication. MX's SLA is nearly identical to Osmind's — both use the standard template. Osmind's 5 business day notification window is unique and more Customer-favorable than the 24-hour standard. Upwind's 90-day notification and automatic credit application remain the absolute ceiling.
